findings.problem: Narrative process leakage appears in diegetic prose ("chapter one of this winter"), which breaks immersion and violates the constitution rule against meta-textual draft/chapter framing inside story reality.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the memory clause at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:116 to an in-world temporal anchor (for example, first week of the freeze or before Christmas) while preserving the wall-signal continuity beat.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if line 116 no longer uses meta-book terms (such as "chapter"/"scene") and the revised sentence still clearly identifies when the wall signal originated in-world; FAIL if any drafting/chapter vocabulary remains in narration.

findings.problem: Clipped narration fragments recur at high-pressure beats ("Still." / "A bed tonight and nothing promised after." / "No guarantee of income..."), producing telegraphic prose that conflicts with the constitution's no-elliptical-clipping rule.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the fragment-heavy lines at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:25, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:207, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:409-410 into grammatically complete close-third sentences that keep urgency but restore full syntactic function.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised spans at lines 25, 207, and 409-410 read as complete sentences with explicit subject+finite verb while preserving the same stakes; FAIL if any of those spans remain as clipped nominal fragments.

findings.problem: The chapter overuses one comparison scaffold ("as if ...") more than twice, creating a recurring sentence formula that flattens rhythmic variance.
findings.rewrite_direction: Target the repeated comparison beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:11, 43, 139, 155, 225, 274, and 369; keep at most two "as if" constructions and convert the others to direct sensory observation, action consequence, or a different syntactic comparison shape.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than two narrative "as if" comparison constructions remain across the cited spans and each revised line communicates the same perception without repeating the original template; FAIL if three or more "as if" templates still appear in those spans.

findings.problem: The central transfer-prep block runs as extended procedural checklist with limited adversarial friction, dropping scene pressure between two stronger confrontation set-pieces.
findings.rewrite_direction: Restructure snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:215-244 so paperwork/inventory beats are compressed and at least one immediate obstacle (timing, missing item, transport threat, or interruption) forces a new decision before the 4:10 time check at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:253.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised 215-253 span contains at least one concrete obstacle that changes what the characters do next in-scene, and no more than two consecutive paragraphs are pure list/form handling without leverage shift; FAIL if the section remains primarily static checklist narration.

findings.problem: Noreen's coached legal phrase is repeated verbatim under peak pressure, which makes her voice sound ventriloquized by procedural language and weakens idiolect separation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one legally explicit line in the showdown at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:341-344, but revise the duplicate repetition so Noreen's second beat uses her own high-contraction, emotionally specific cadence while preserving the same legal intent.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if lines 341-344 contain the legal claim once and a distinct follow-up line in Noreen's own register (including at least one contraction and one concrete stake); FAIL if the coached sentence is repeated verbatim multiple times in that span.

findings.problem: Peter's stress tell is rendered with near-redundant formality labels across multiple beats, reading like over-literal style-bible token reuse rather than fresh scene-specific observation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the repeated formality markers at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:82, 295, and 353 so only one beat names formality abstractly; in the other beats, show the shift through concrete diction choices, interruption patterns, or observable effect on Noreen/others.
findings.acceptance_test: PASS if among lines 82, 295, and 353 only one line uses an explicit abstract formality label and the other two communicate the same shift via concrete speech/action cues; FAIL if all three beats still rely on explicit label-style narration.

summary: Chapter 16 lands its major state change and core causality, but it does not clear pass-level craft rigor due process leakage, clipped-fragment prose, repeated sentence formulas, a mid-scene momentum dip, and voice flattening in key dialogue beats.
